comments: find(pat, s) returns Just(t) where t is the leftmost substring of s matching pat, or Nothing if pat does not match anywhere in s.

Regex syntax is host-defined; behavior tends to converge on the intersection of ECMA-262 and POSIX-ERE features (literal characters, character classes, alternation, anchors ^ and $, repetition with ?/*/+/{n,m}, grouping with parentheses), but extension features (lookaround, backreferences, Unicode property classes, named groups, engine-specific flags) vary widely. For portable code, restrict patterns to the common subset.
Total in the sense that no error is raised at the kernel level; behavior on an ill-formed pattern is host-defined.
metadata.description: Find the first regex match within a string, returning the matched substring if any.

comments: findAll(pat, s) returns the list of all leftmost, non-overlapping matches of pat in s, in the order they appear. Returns the empty list if pat does not match anywhere.
Regex syntax is host-defined; see find for the common-subset caveat.
Total; ill-formed patterns are host-defined.
metadata.description: Find all non-overlapping regex matches within a string.

comments: matches(pat, s) returns true iff pat matches somewhere in s (not anchored to the start or end; for whole-string matching, anchor the pattern explicitly with ^ and $).
Regex syntax is host-defined; see find for the common-subset caveat.
Total; ill-formed patterns are host-defined.
metadata.description: Test whether a regex matches anywhere in a string.

comments: replace(pat, repl, s) returns s with the first leftmost match of pat replaced by repl. If pat does not match, s is returned unchanged.
Replacement-string syntax (capture-group references such as $1 or \\1, literal escapes) is host-defined.
Regex syntax is host-defined; see find for the common-subset caveat.
Total; ill-formed patterns and replacement strings are host-defined.
metadata.description: Replace the first regex match in a string with a replacement string.

comments: replaceAll(pat, repl, s) returns s with every leftmost, non-overlapping match of pat replaced by repl. If pat does not match anywhere, s is returned unchanged.
Replacement-string syntax is host-defined; see replace and find for the common-subset caveats.
Total; ill-formed patterns and replacement strings are host-defined.
metadata.description: Replace all non-overlapping regex matches in a string with a replacement string.

comments: split(pat, s) returns the list of substrings of s obtained by splitting on every leftmost, non-overlapping match of pat.
Trailing empty splits are host-defined (some engines retain them, some discard them; for portable code, do not rely on the trailing-empty behavior).
Regex syntax is host-defined; see find for the common-subset caveat.
Total; ill-formed patterns are host-defined.
metadata.description: Split a string by occurrences of a regex pattern.
